title: Review round 2: sieve-form post-fix-wave-1
notes:
SEC0 verdict: ISSUES-FOUND — validate_stmt unconditionally skips i+2 on :comparator, allowing invalid regex patterns to bypass compile-time validation.
SEC7: Running out of real complaints: NO. Confidence: 20% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: (1) The rposition-based 'last Str/StringList' heuristic in validate_regex_in_clause and cache_patterns_in_clause — correctness depends on the Sieve grammar always placing the key-list last; counter-examples with multi-argument tests or future extensions could break this silently. (2) The bare_address rfind('<') approach does not handle nested angle brackets or comments per RFC 5322; the impact is bounded to address tests on malformed headers but could cause false negatives. (3) expand_vars always allocates a new String even when no substitution occurs (the Borrowed branch is taken but the owned branch does not check whether any substitution actually happened before pushing to the output). Uncertainty: no fuzzing or property-based testing visible; regex ReDoS properties of fancy-regex on untrusted patterns not audited; no test coverage for not-test or nested anyof/allof; the stop command interaction with Keep default is tested but the StmtResult::Stop vs None distinction in eval_script is subtle.
